name: DJ Moore
headline: Allen's new toy buys back Moore's WR1 ceiling
body:
Moore's 2025 was a study in collapse: a WR39 finish on just 682 yards, a 16% target share, and a career-low 1.30 yards per route as Chicago funneled work to Rome Odunze and Colston Loveland. His 0.162 targets per route and 45.9% success rate were both shadows of the 0.252 and 57.4% he posted in 2023, when he ranked sixth at the position with 1,364 yards and a sterling 0.487 EPA per play.

Buffalo flips the variable that mattered most. Pairing with Josh Allen reopens the explosive ceiling Moore showed at peak, even if a run-leaning attack and a crowded room cap his volume near the double-digit-target range he saw last year. The red-zone usage is thin and the floor is volatile, but the talent and quarterback upgrade make him a defensible Round 5 swing on rebound equity.
